evals.prompt: Work on the next claude issue
evals.expected_output: Runs gh issue list to find open issues labeled 'claude', picks the highest-priority one (by priority labels), writes subtask checkboxes into the issue body, creates a branch named <number>-<slug>, implements the work following TDD, checks off each subtask as it's done, and opens a PR with a summary that includes 'Closes #<number>'.

assertions.text: The skill runs a gh issue list command filtering for the 'claude' label
The skill selects the issue with the highest priority label (P0 > P1 > P2 > P3/unlabeled)
The skill edits the GitHub issue body to add task-list checkboxes (- [ ] subtask)
The created branch name includes the issue number
The PR body includes 'Closes #<number>' to auto-link the issue
The PR description contains a summary section describing what was built

evals.prompt: Pick up the next claude issue and continue working
evals.expected_output: Finds an in-progress issue (branch exists, some subtasks already checked off, no open PR), checks out the existing branch, reads which subtasks remain, completes those subtasks, and creates a PR with a summary covering all changes across the entire effort.

assertions.text: The skill detects that a branch already exists for this issue and checks it out rather than creating a new one
The skill reads the issue body and identifies which subtasks are already done vs remaining
The skill only implements the unchecked subtasks — it does not redo already-completed work
A PR is created after all subtasks are complete

evals.expected_output: Finds a draft PR for an issue labeled 'claude' that has review comments, checks out the PR branch, addresses each comment with code changes or explanatory replies, pushes the changes, then converts the PR from draft to ready using 'gh pr ready', and posts a summary comment.

assertions.text: The skill identifies that the issue already has a draft PR with review comments (State C)
The skill uses gh pr checkout to switch to the PR's branch
Each review comment is addressed either via a code change or an explanatory reply on the PR
The skill runs 'gh pr ready <number>' to convert the draft PR to a ready-for-review PR
After fixing review comments, each one is marked as resolved (or replied to with a ✅) so the reviewer knows it was addressed
A summary comment listing all changes made is posted to the PR after promotion
